industries.name: Phosphors & Displays
uses: Yttrium oxide (Y₂O₃) host lattice doped with europium (Eu³⁺) produces the brilliant red phosphor in fluorescent lamps, color CRT televisions, and white LEDs — Y₂O₃:Eu is the standard red component in tricolor fluorescent lamp phosphor blends

Yttrium aluminum garnet (YAG, Y₃Al₅O₁₂) doped with cerium (Ce³⁺) converts blue GaN LED emission to broad yellow-green spectrum — YAG:Ce is the dominant phosphor in white LED lighting worldwide, used in billions of LED bulbs and backlights
Y₂O₃:Eu red phosphor in plasma display panels (PDPs) and projection TV systems — largely obsolete as LCD and OLED have displaced plasma and CRT
Yttrium vanadate (YVO₄:Eu) red phosphor for fluorescent lamps — higher efficiency and color purity than Y₂O₃:Eu in tricolor compact fluorescent lamps
Scintillator crystals: yttrium aluminum perovskite (YAP) and gadolinium yttrium silicate for gamma ray and X-ray detection in medical imaging (PET scanners) and high-energy physics detectors

companies.name: Nichia Corporation
companies.use: World's largest LED manufacturer and major YAG:Ce phosphor producer. Developed and patented YAG:Ce white LED phosphor technology in 1996. Produces YAG:Ce for its LED packages and supplies phosphor to other LED manufacturers.

companies.name: Intematix (Enphys Acquisition)
companies.use: Major LED phosphor manufacturer producing YAG:Ce, nitride red, and specialty phosphors for white LED lighting, display backlighting, and automotive LED applications globally.

companies.name: Merck KGaA (Electronics)
companies.use: Produces specialty phosphors including YAG:Ce and other yttrium-based phosphors for LED lighting, display, and scientific detector applications. Operates phosphor synthesis and coating operations in Germany and globally.

industries.name: Specialty Ceramics & Refractories
uses: Yttria-stabilized zirconia (YSZ, 3–8 mol% Y₂O₃ in ZrO₂): prevents the destructive tetragonal-to-monoclinic phase transformation on cooling, producing tough, wear-resistant zirconia ceramics for dental crowns, knife blades, watch cases, and pump components

YSZ thermal barrier coatings (TBC) on nickel superalloy turbine blades by electron beam physical vapor deposition (EB-PVD) or atmospheric plasma spray — reduces blade temperature by 150–300°C, enabling higher turbine inlet temperatures and greater engine efficiency
YSZ electrolyte in solid oxide fuel cells (SOFCs) — cubic-stabilized YSZ conducts oxide ions (O²⁻) at 800–1000°C for electrochemical power generation with 60%+ electrical efficiency
Yttrium oxide crucibles and furnace liners for melting reactive metals (uranium, thorium, plutonium) — Y₂O₃ is chemically inert to most molten metals at high temperature
Y₂O₃ additions to aluminum, magnesium, and titanium alloys improve high-temperature oxidation resistance and creep properties in gas turbine components and aerospace structures

companies.name: Saint-Gobain
companies.use: Major producer of YSZ thermal barrier coating powders, YSZ electrolyte discs for solid oxide fuel cells, and yttria-stabilized zirconia grinding media and wear components for industrial applications.

companies.name: Tosoh Corporation
companies.use: World's largest producer of yttria-stabilized zirconia powder for dental ceramic applications. Its TZ-3YE and multi-layer zirconia powders are sintered into dental crowns, bridges, and implant abutments by dental labs globally.

companies.name: Oerlikon Metco
companies.use: Major supplier of YSZ thermal barrier coating powders and plasma spray equipment for gas turbine blade coating. Supplies GE Aviation, Pratt & Whitney, Rolls-Royce, and Siemens Energy with TBC coating services and materials.

industries.name: Lasers & Medical
uses: Nd:YAG (neodymium-doped yttrium aluminum garnet) laser: the workhorse solid-state laser emitting at 1064nm — used for laser cutting and welding of metals, dental procedures, ophthalmology (YAG capsulotomy for posterior capsule opacification after cataract surgery), tattoo removal, and hair removal
Er:YAG (erbium:YAG) laser at 2940nm for dental cavity preparation, skin resurfacing, and surgical tissue ablation — water absorption at 2940nm produces precise, low-thermal-damage tissue removal
Ho:YAG (holmium:YAG) at 2100nm for kidney stone fragmentation (lithotripsy), prostate surgery (HoLEP), and orthopedic arthroscopy delivered through flexible optical fibers

Yb:YAG (ytterbium:YAG) thin-disk and fiber lasers at 1030nm for high-power industrial cutting and welding — high efficiency (>30% wall plug), excellent beam quality, compact format replacing CO₂ lasers in many metalworking applications
Q-switched Nd:YAG at 1064nm and frequency-doubled 532nm for tattoo removal, pigmented lesion treatment, and onychomycosis (nail fungus) laser therapy

companies.name: Coherent (II-VI)
companies.use: Major manufacturer of Nd:YAG, Er:YAG, and Yb:YAG laser systems for industrial, medical, and scientific applications. Produces YAG crystal boules and finished laser gain media in addition to complete laser systems.

companies.name: Trumpf
companies.use: World's largest industrial laser manufacturer. Produces Yb:YAG disk lasers (TruDisk) and Yb fiber lasers for sheet metal cutting, welding, and additive manufacturing. Major consumer of yttrium in YAG crystal gain media.

companies.name: Cynosure (Hologic)
companies.use: Major medical aesthetic laser company producing Nd:YAG and Er:YAG laser systems for tattoo removal, skin rejuvenation, hair removal, and pigmented lesion treatment in dermatology and aesthetics clinics globally.
